english: and infidels, in which the latter will be destroyed, is widely spread through Tibet.
Wdṅ. n. of a plant, perhaps kećuka (ke cu ka), Arum Colocasia, with edible root; or = keṅ-śu-ka (keng shu ka)?
= ཀག་མ་ kág-ma (kag ma) Cs.
skeleton.
Lex., Sambh., Wdṅ., n. of a tree.
also ke-rtse (ke rtse), jacket Mil
n. of a female terrifying deity Thgr.
Dzl. xi, 1: keu-lei rgya (ke'u le'i rgya), acc. to the Mongol version: customary seal, — dubious.
suddenly Sch.
to raise, lift up, e. g. the finger towards heaven Glr.; ker laṅ-ba (ker lang ba) to rise, stand up.
1. num.: 121; kó-pa (ko pa) the 121st (volume). — 2. affix, = ka (ka) col. Ld — 3. all, whole Schr., cf. kob (kob).
1. also ko-sko (ko sko), throat, chin Sch., ko-sko degs (ko sko degs) Lex.? 2. = ka-ka (ka ka) W.
n. of a country in or near Ceylon Pth

also ko-nyot-tsé (ko nyot tse), ko-lon-tsé (ko lon tse) the kernel of the pine-apple Cs.; more particularly the edible seed of the Neosa-pine in the valley of the Sutledj; also skaṅ-nyan-tsé (skang nyan tse) Kun.
Ssk. कोट, a kind of leprosy Wdṅ.
a sort of tea Schr.
guitar Ld.; it is tuned in 3 fourths.

1. hide, skin. — 2. leather, *kó-wa nyé-k'an* (ko wa nye khan) tanner C.; gláṅ-ko (glang ko) neat's leather. — ko-krád (ko krad) leather-shoe. — kó-mkan (ko mkan) 1. tanner. 2. (acc. to some also:) conductor of a leather-boat, boatman. — ko-btúm (ko btum) 'leather-wrapping' is said to be a criminal punishment in C., in different degrees of severity, e. g. *lág-pa ko-túm* (lag pa ko tum), when the culprit's hands are cut off, the stumps sewed up in leather, and the wretch thrown as a beggar upon public charity etc. — ko-t'ág (ko thag) strap, thong. — ko-t'ágs (ko thags) Cs.: a small instrument of leather to weave lace with. — ko-gdán (ko gdan) a piece of leather put under the saddle Sch. — ko-lpágs (ko lpags) hide, leather. — ko-búgs (ko bugs) Sch.: three-edged needle for leather. — ko-ts'ág (ko tshag) leather-sieve. — ko-rúl (ko rul) a rotten hide.
n. of a country Wdk.
n. of a bird Wdṅ.
is meant to express the voice of a raven.
Hindi कोरा, more tibetanized ko-rás (ko ras), unbleached coarse cotton cloth.
in compounds kor (kor) W., cup for drinking; śiṅ-kor (shing kor) wooden cup, a utensil every Tibetan carries with him in his bosom; śel-kor (shel kor) (European) tumbler. (Cf. pór-pa (por pa)).
a dubious word. Sch. has ko-loṅ-ba (ko long ba) to hate, envy, but in a passage in Mil., where the connection admits of no doubt, ko-loṅ mdzad-pa (ko long mdzad pa) must be taken for: to disdain.
Dzl., Glr., Ssk. कौशाम्बी n. of an ancient city on the Ganges, in the Doab.
Sambh., Ssk.: कोसला, = Ayodhya, Oude.

I. subst., also skóg-pa (skog pa), skógs-pa (skogs pa) shell, peel, rind; śún-kog (shun kog) id.; p'yí-kog (phyi kog) exterior shell, bark; kóg-pa śu-ba (kog pa shu ba) to peel, pare. — II. vb. n. to splinter off, to chink; kog laṅ-ba (kog lang ba) 1. id. 2. to rise suddenly and run away.

koṅ (kong), also koṅ-kóṅ (kong kong), 1. concave, excavated. — 2. crooked, *p'i-śi tsíg-pa koṅ-kóṅ co* (phi shi tsig pa kong kong co) the cat makes a crooked back W. — kóṅ-po (kong po) 1. cup, bowl. 2. crucible. 3. breach, gap Sch. 4. n. of a province S. E. of Lhasa. — koṅ-bu (kong bu) small cup, bowl. — mčód-kóṅ (mchod kong) offering-bowl; snág-koṅ (snag kong) inkstand
